info.title: Traigent API Key Management Routes
info.description: Canonical backend routes for API key lifecycle management (POST /api/v1/keys). Backed by TraigentBackend/src/routes/auth_routes.py key handlers.

post.description: Create a new API key for the authenticated user. The plaintext secret is returned once in the 201 response (create_secret field) and never again — the client must store it immediately. Only user-requestable scopes are accepted; admin:all is a privileged server-side scope and is rejected. Keys may optionally be scoped to a project_id; the caller must be a project member or admin to create project-scoped keys.

201.description: API key created successfully. The create_secret field contains the one-time plaintext secret.

400.description: Bad request — malformed body, invalid scope tokens, or name too long.

401.description: Not authenticated — missing or invalid session/token.

403.description: Forbidden — only project members or admins may create project-scoped keys; or the requested scopes exceed the caller's entitlements.

409.description: Conflict — a key with the same name already exists for this user.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.
